scenarioDescription: A multinational corporation is migrating 500 on-premises applications to AWS over 18 months. They need to discover application dependencies, group applications into migration waves, track migration progress across teams, and select the appropriate migration strategy (6 Rs) for each workload based on complexity and business value.
toolboxServices: AWS Application Discovery Service|AWS Migration Hub|AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)|AWS Migration Evaluator|Amazon Inspector|AWS CloudFormation|AWS Transfer Family
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Migration Evaluator|AWS Application Discovery Service|AWS Migration Hub|AWS Application Migration Service (MGN)
hint: Before discovering dependencies, you need to build a business case with TCO analysis to justify the migration investment.
hint2: Application Discovery Service agents collect dependency data that Migration Hub uses to group applications into migration waves.
explanation: AWS Migration Evaluator builds the initial business case with TCO analysis and projected AWS costs to justify the migration. AWS Application Discovery Service deploys agents on-premises to collect server specifications, utilization data, and network dependency mappings between the 500 applications. AWS Migration Hub aggregates discovery data, enables grouping applications into migration waves based on dependencies, and tracks progress across all teams with a centralized dashboard. AWS Application Migration Service (MGN) executes the actual lift-and-shift rehosting with continuous block-level replication and minimal-downtime cutover.

scenarioDescription: A global SaaS company needs to design a multi-region active-active architecture for their customer-facing API. Each region must serve read and write traffic independently, data must be eventually consistent across regions within 1 second, and the system must automatically route users to the nearest healthy region with instant failover.
toolboxServices: Amazon DynamoDB Global Tables|AWS Global Accelerator|Amazon API Gateway|Amazon Aurora Global Database|Amazon Route 53|Amazon CloudFront|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Global Accelerator|Amazon API Gateway|Amazon DynamoDB Global Tables
hint: Global Accelerator provides static anycast IPs that route users to the nearest region with health-check-based failover in seconds — faster than DNS.
hint2: DynamoDB Global Tables provide multi-region active-active replication with sub-second consistency, supporting independent writes in each region.
explanation: AWS Global Accelerator provides static anycast IP addresses that automatically route users to the nearest healthy region with sub-second failover — no DNS TTL propagation delays. Amazon API Gateway in each region handles the REST API with regional endpoints, authentication, throttling, and Lambda/ECS backend integration. Amazon DynamoDB Global Tables provide multi-region active-active replication with sub-second eventual consistency, enabling independent read and write operations in each region with automatic conflict resolution using last-writer-wins.

scenarioDescription: A healthcare company needs to build a HIPAA-compliant data lake that ingests HL7/FHIR data from 200 hospital systems, de-identifies PHI for research datasets, enforces column-level access control so researchers only see approved data elements, and provides SQL query access without managing infrastructure.
toolboxServices: AWS Lake Formation|Amazon S3|AWS Glue|Amazon Athena|Amazon Macie|Amazon Comprehend Medical|Amazon Redshift
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|Amazon Comprehend Medical|AWS Glue|AWS Lake Formation|Amazon Athena
hint: Raw HL7/FHIR data lands in S3 first, then a purpose-built NLP service extracts and de-identifies medical entities from unstructured clinical text.
hint2: Lake Formation provides column-level and cell-level security that controls exactly which data elements each researcher can query via Athena.
explanation: Amazon S3 ingests and stores raw HL7/FHIR data from 200 hospital systems as the central data lake storage layer. Amazon Comprehend Medical extracts medical entities (diagnoses, medications, procedures) and de-identifies PHI by detecting and redacting protected health information from clinical text. AWS Glue ETL jobs transform the de-identified data into optimized Parquet format with the Glue Data Catalog maintaining schema metadata. AWS Lake Formation enforces fine-grained column-level and cell-level access controls ensuring researchers only see approved data elements. Amazon Athena provides serverless SQL queries over the governed data lake without infrastructure management.

scenarioDescription: A financial services company needs to implement a landing zone for 300+ accounts with preventive and detective guardrails, automated account provisioning with customized networking blueprints, federated SSO access, and centralized governance — while allowing individual business units to manage their own workloads independently.
toolboxServices: AWS Control Tower|AWS Organizations|AWS SSO (IAM Identity Center)|AWS CloudFormation StackSets|AWS Service Catalog|AWS Config|AWS Firewall Manager
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Control Tower|AWS Organizations|AWS CloudFormation StackSets|AWS SSO (IAM Identity Center)|AWS Config
hint: Control Tower establishes the landing zone with pre-configured guardrails, centralized logging, and the foundational multi-account structure.
hint2: StackSets deploy customized networking blueprints (VPCs, Transit Gateway attachments, DNS) to new accounts via Control Tower lifecycle hooks.
explanation: AWS Control Tower establishes the landing zone with mandatory guardrails (SCPs for preventive, Config rules for detective), centralized logging (CloudTrail, Config), and the foundational OU structure. AWS Organizations provides the hierarchical account structure with SCPs enforcing permission boundaries at each OU level for business unit isolation. AWS CloudFormation StackSets deploy customized networking blueprints (VPCs, Transit Gateway attachments, Route 53 Resolver rules) to each new account via Control Tower Account Factory lifecycle hooks. AWS SSO (IAM Identity Center) provides federated access with permission sets mapped to OUs, enabling business units to manage their own workloads. AWS Config provides continuous compliance monitoring with organization-wide conformance packs.

scenarioDescription: A media streaming company needs to transcode uploaded videos into adaptive bitrate formats (HLS/DASH), generate thumbnails, extract metadata, detect inappropriate content, and deliver the processed content globally with sub-100ms latency — all triggered automatically when creators upload raw video files.
toolboxServices: Amazon S3|AWS Lambda|AWS Step Functions|AWS Elemental MediaConvert|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon CloudFront|Amazon Elastic Transcoder
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|AWS Step Functions|AWS Elemental MediaConvert|Amazon Rekognition|Amazon CloudFront
hint: S3 event notifications trigger the workflow when a new video is uploaded to the raw content bucket.
hint2: Step Functions orchestrates the parallel processing branches — transcoding, thumbnail generation, content moderation — with error handling and retry logic.
explanation: Amazon S3 receives the raw video upload and triggers an S3 event notification. AWS Step Functions orchestrates the multi-step media processing workflow with parallel branches for transcoding, thumbnail generation, and content moderation — including error handling, retries, and conditional logic. AWS Elemental MediaConvert transcodes the video into adaptive bitrate HLS/DASH formats with multiple quality renditions. Amazon Rekognition Content Moderation analyzes video frames to detect inappropriate content, flagging videos that violate community guidelines before publication. Amazon CloudFront delivers the processed content globally from edge locations with sub-100ms latency.

scenarioDescription: A company is running a monolithic .NET application on a single large EC2 instance. They need to decompose it into microservices, containerize each service, implement service discovery, manage inter-service communication with mTLS, and deploy with canary releases — while minimizing operational overhead.
toolboxServices: Amazon ECS Fargate|AWS App Mesh|AWS Cloud Map|Amazon ECR|AWS App Runner|Amazon EKS|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon ECR|Amazon ECS Fargate|AWS Cloud Map|AWS App Mesh|Elastic Load Balancing (ALB)
hint: Container images must be stored in a registry before they can be deployed to any container orchestration service.
hint2: Cloud Map provides service discovery that App Mesh uses to dynamically locate and route traffic between microservices.
explanation: Amazon ECR stores the container images for each decomposed microservice with vulnerability scanning and lifecycle policies. Amazon ECS Fargate runs the containerized microservices without managing EC2 instances, providing the lowest operational overhead for container orchestration. AWS Cloud Map provides service discovery, automatically registering each Fargate task's endpoint so services can locate each other dynamically. AWS App Mesh deploys Envoy sidecar proxies alongside each task for mTLS encryption, traffic routing (canary releases with weighted targets), and observability — all without application code changes. Elastic Load Balancing (ALB) serves as the ingress point with path-based routing to the appropriate microservices.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a hybrid DNS architecture where on-premises applications can resolve AWS private hosted zone records, AWS applications can resolve on-premises Active Directory DNS zones, and all DNS queries must be logged and analyzed for security threats.
toolboxServices: Amazon Route 53 Resolver|Route 53 Resolver Inbound Endpoints|Route 53 Resolver Outbound Endpoints|Amazon Route 53|AWS Direct Connect|Amazon CloudWatch Logs|AWS Network Firewall
correctPipelineOrder: Route 53 Resolver Inbound Endpoints|Route 53 Resolver Outbound Endpoints|Amazon Route 53 Resolver|Amazon CloudWatch Logs
hint: Inbound endpoints allow on-premises DNS resolvers to forward queries into AWS VPCs for private hosted zone resolution.
hint2: Outbound endpoints allow AWS resources to forward DNS queries to on-premises Active Directory DNS servers for corporate domain resolution.
explanation: Route 53 Resolver Inbound Endpoints create ENIs in the VPC that on-premises DNS servers forward queries to, enabling resolution of AWS private hosted zone records (e.g., internal.company.aws) from on-premises applications. Route 53 Resolver Outbound Endpoints forward DNS queries from AWS resources to on-premises Active Directory DNS servers via conditional forwarding rules for corporate domains (e.g., corp.company.com). Amazon Route 53 Resolver manages the forwarding rules that determine which queries go to which DNS servers based on domain name matching. Amazon CloudWatch Logs receives Route 53 Resolver query logs for all DNS queries, enabling security analysis and threat detection of DNS-based attacks.

scenarioDescription: A retail company processes 50,000 orders per minute during flash sales. They need to guarantee exactly-once processing of each order, handle downstream service failures gracefully with automatic retries, maintain order processing sequence per customer, and scale to handle 10x traffic spikes without data loss.
toolboxServices: Amazon SQS FIFO|Amazon SNS|AWS Lambda|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon MQ|AWS Step Functions
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon SNS|Amazon SQS FIFO|AWS Lambda|Amazon DynamoDB
hint: SNS fans out the order event to multiple SQS FIFO queues — one per downstream processing domain (inventory, payment, fulfillment).
hint2: SQS FIFO queues with MessageGroupId set to customer ID guarantee per-customer ordering with exactly-once processing via deduplication.
explanation: Amazon SNS receives the order event and fans it out to multiple Amazon SQS FIFO queue subscriptions for independent downstream processing (inventory, payment, fulfillment). Amazon SQS FIFO queues guarantee exactly-once processing via content-based deduplication and maintain per-customer ordering using MessageGroupId set to the customer ID — with built-in retry and dead-letter queue handling for downstream failures. AWS Lambda processes messages from each FIFO queue with reserved concurrency matching the number of message groups, scaling to handle traffic spikes while maintaining ordering guarantees. Amazon DynamoDB stores the processed order state with conditional writes preventing duplicate processing at the persistence layer.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a centralized egress architecture for 100 VPCs. All internet-bound traffic must be inspected by a next-generation firewall, NAT'd through a shared pool of Elastic IPs for partner allowlisting, and logged for compliance — while minimizing the number of NAT gateways and firewall instances.
toolboxServices: AWS Transit Gateway|AWS Network Firewall|NAT Gateway|Amazon VPC|AWS Firewall Manager|AWS PrivateLink|Internet Gateway
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Transit Gateway|AWS Network Firewall|NAT Gateway|Internet Gateway
hint: Transit Gateway centralizes routing from 100 spoke VPCs to a shared inspection VPC, eliminating the need for per-VPC firewalls and NAT gateways.
hint2: Network Firewall inspects traffic before it reaches the NAT Gateway, enabling deep packet inspection and domain filtering on egress traffic.
explanation: AWS Transit Gateway acts as the central hub, routing all internet-bound traffic from 100 spoke VPCs to a shared centralized egress/inspection VPC — eliminating the need for per-VPC NAT gateways and firewalls. AWS Network Firewall in the inspection VPC performs stateful deep packet inspection, domain filtering, and IPS/IDS on all egress traffic before it reaches the internet. NAT Gateway translates private IPs to a shared pool of Elastic IPs (for partner allowlisting) in the egress VPC. The Internet Gateway provides the final hop to the internet, with VPC Flow Logs and Network Firewall logs providing the compliance audit trail.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to migrate a 100 TB Oracle Data Warehouse to Amazon Redshift. The migration must convert Oracle-specific SQL and stored procedures, transfer the data with minimal downtime, validate data integrity post-migration, and optimize the Redshift schema for columnar storage performance.
toolboxServices: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)|AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)|Amazon Redshift|AWS Snowball Edge|Amazon S3|AWS Glue|AWS DataSync
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT)|AWS Snowball Edge|Amazon S3|AWS Database Migration Service (DMS)|Amazon Redshift
hint: SCT converts Oracle-specific SQL, stored procedures, and schema definitions to Redshift-compatible syntax before any data moves.
hint2: For 100 TB, Snowball Edge provides faster bulk transfer than network-based migration, with DMS handling ongoing CDC replication for minimal downtime.
explanation: AWS Schema Conversion Tool (SCT) converts the Oracle Data Warehouse schema — including Oracle-specific SQL, PL/SQL stored procedures, and data types — to Amazon Redshift-compatible syntax with optimization recommendations for columnar storage (sort keys, distribution keys). AWS Snowball Edge performs the initial bulk transfer of 100 TB (faster than network transfer at this scale), loading data to Amazon S3 as a staging area. AWS Database Migration Service (DMS) handles ongoing Change Data Capture (CDC) replication from the live Oracle source to keep Redshift synchronized during the migration window. Amazon Redshift receives the migrated data with the SCT-optimized schema, and post-migration validation compares row counts, checksums, and query results between source and target.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a zero-trust network architecture for their AWS workloads. Every request must be authenticated and authorized regardless of network location, lateral movement between services must be prevented, and all access decisions must be logged and continuously evaluated.
toolboxServices: AWS Verified Access|AWS IAM Identity Center|Amazon VPC Lattice|AWS WAF|AWS PrivateLink|Amazon GuardDuty|AWS Network Firewall
correctPipelineOrder: AWS IAM Identity Center|AWS Verified Access|Amazon VPC Lattice|Amazon GuardDuty
hint: Verified Access evaluates each request against identity and device trust policies before granting access — replacing traditional VPN-based network perimeter security.
hint2: VPC Lattice provides service-to-service authentication and authorization with IAM auth policies, preventing unauthorized lateral movement between microservices.
explanation: AWS IAM Identity Center provides the centralized identity foundation with MFA and device trust signals that feed into access decisions. AWS Verified Access evaluates every user request against identity-based and device-posture trust policies before granting access to applications — eliminating the need for VPNs and implementing per-request zero-trust verification. Amazon VPC Lattice enforces service-to-service zero-trust by requiring IAM authentication and authorization policies for every inter-service call, preventing lateral movement even within the same VPC. Amazon GuardDuty continuously monitors all network flows, API calls, and DNS queries to detect anomalous access patterns and potential compromises in real-time.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a multi-account CI/CD pipeline where developers push code in workload accounts, but all builds, security scans, and deployments are orchestrated from a central tooling account. Artifacts must be encrypted with a customer-managed KMS key accessible across accounts, and deployments must use blue/green strategy with automatic rollback.
toolboxServices: AWS CodePipeline|AWS CodeBuild|AWS CodeDeploy|AWS KMS|Amazon ECR|AWS CloudFormation|AWS CodeCommit
correctPipelineOrder: AWS CodeCommit|AWS CodePipeline|AWS CodeBuild|AWS KMS|AWS CodeDeploy
hint: CodePipeline in the central tooling account uses cross-account IAM roles to access CodeCommit repositories in workload accounts.
hint2: KMS customer-managed keys with cross-account key policies encrypt all pipeline artifacts, ensuring workload accounts can decrypt during deployment.
explanation: AWS CodeCommit in workload accounts hosts the source code, triggering cross-account EventBridge events to the central tooling account. AWS CodePipeline in the tooling account orchestrates the entire workflow using cross-account IAM roles to access resources in workload accounts. AWS CodeBuild runs builds, unit tests, and security scans (SAST/DAST/container scanning) in the tooling account's isolated build environment. AWS KMS customer-managed keys encrypt all pipeline artifacts (build outputs, container images) with cross-account key policies granting decrypt permissions to workload account deployment roles. AWS CodeDeploy executes blue/green deployments in workload accounts with automatic rollback triggered by CloudWatch alarm thresholds on error rates and latency.

scenarioDescription: A company runs SAP HANA on-premises and needs to migrate to AWS. The database requires 6 TB of memory, consistent sub-millisecond storage latency, and high availability with automatic failover. The migration must be certified by SAP and minimize downtime to under 4 hours.
toolboxServices: Amazon EC2 High Memory Instances|Amazon EBS io2 Block Express|AWS Launch Wizard for SAP|Amazon FSx for NetApp ONTAP|AWS Backint Agent|Amazon EFS|AWS Application Migration Service
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Launch Wizard for SAP|Amazon EC2 High Memory Instances|Amazon EBS io2 Block Express|AWS Backint Agent
hint: Launch Wizard for SAP automates the deployment of SAP-certified infrastructure following AWS and SAP best practices with built-in HA configuration.
hint2: EC2 High Memory instances (u-6tb1.metal) provide the 6 TB of memory required for SAP HANA in a single instance with bare metal performance.
explanation: AWS Launch Wizard for SAP automates the deployment of SAP-certified infrastructure — configuring VPCs, subnets, security groups, and SAP HANA cluster settings following AWS and SAP best practices with built-in high availability using SUSE/RHEL clustering and Pacemaker. Amazon EC2 High Memory Instances (u-6tb1.metal) provide 6 TB of dedicated memory on bare metal for SAP HANA certification requirements. Amazon EBS io2 Block Express delivers consistent sub-millisecond latency with up to 256,000 IOPS and 4,000 MB/s throughput for the HANA data and log volumes. AWS Backint Agent for SAP HANA provides SAP-certified backup directly to S3, enabling HANA system replication-based migration with under 4 hours of downtime during final cutover.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a data mesh architecture where each business domain (sales, marketing, logistics) owns and publishes their data as products. Consumers must discover available data products, request access through a self-service portal, and query data across domains without copying it — all with centralized governance and decentralized ownership.
toolboxServices: AWS Lake Formation|AWS Glue Data Catalog|Amazon Athena|Amazon DataZone|Amazon Redshift|AWS RAM|Amazon S3
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|AWS Glue Data Catalog|Amazon DataZone|AWS Lake Formation|Amazon Athena
hint: DataZone provides the business data catalog and self-service portal where domain teams publish data products and consumers discover and request access.
hint2: Lake Formation enforces fine-grained access controls (column/row/cell level) that DataZone access grants translate into, maintaining centralized governance.
explanation: Amazon S3 stores each domain's data in their respective AWS accounts, maintaining decentralized data ownership. AWS Glue Data Catalog maintains the technical metadata (schemas, partitions, statistics) for each domain's datasets. Amazon DataZone provides the business data catalog where domain teams publish data products with business metadata, and consumers discover, request, and receive access through a self-service portal with approval workflows. AWS Lake Formation enforces the fine-grained access controls (column-level, row-level, cell-level security) that DataZone access grants translate into, maintaining centralized governance across the mesh. Amazon Athena enables consumers to query data products across domains using federated queries without copying data between accounts.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a multi-region disaster recovery solution for their entire AWS environment spanning 50 accounts. The solution must continuously replicate EC2 instances, automate recovery orchestration with defined boot order and dependencies, validate recovery regularly, and achieve an RTO of 15 minutes.
toolboxServices: AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (DRS)|AWS CloudFormation StackSets|Amazon Route 53|AWS Step Functions|AWS Backup|Amazon EventBridge|AWS Resilience Hub
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Resilience Hub|AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (DRS)|AWS Step Functions|Amazon Route 53
hint: Resilience Hub first assesses your application's resiliency posture, defines RTO/RPO targets, and identifies gaps before implementing DR.
hint2: Elastic Disaster Recovery continuously replicates EC2 instances to the DR region with sub-second RPO using block-level replication.
explanation: AWS Resilience Hub assesses the resiliency posture of all applications across 50 accounts, defines RTO/RPO targets, identifies single points of failure, and generates recovery recommendations — establishing the DR baseline. AWS Elastic Disaster Recovery (DRS) continuously replicates EC2 instances from the primary region to the DR region using lightweight agents for block-level replication with sub-second RPO and automated instance right-sizing. AWS Step Functions orchestrates the recovery workflow with defined boot order and inter-service dependencies — launching databases before application servers, configuring load balancers, and validating health checks in sequence. Amazon Route 53 failover routing with health checks automatically redirects traffic to the DR region once Step Functions confirms all services are healthy, achieving the 15-minute RTO.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a secure, scalable API platform that serves 10,000 third-party developers. APIs must support usage plans with rate limiting per developer tier, API key management, request/response transformation, caching for read-heavy endpoints, and monetization through a developer portal with self-service subscription.
toolboxServices: Amazon API Gateway|Amazon CloudFront|Amazon Cognito|AWS WAF|Amazon DynamoDB|AWS Lambda|Amazon API Gateway Developer Portal
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon CloudFront|AWS WAF|Amazon API Gateway|AWS Lambda|Amazon DynamoDB
hint: CloudFront in front of API Gateway provides edge caching for read-heavy endpoints and DDoS protection at the network edge.
hint2: API Gateway usage plans with API keys enforce per-developer-tier rate limiting (throttling) and quota management for monetization.
explanation: Amazon CloudFront provides edge caching for read-heavy API endpoints, reducing origin load and latency for 10,000 developers globally, while also providing DDoS protection. AWS WAF attached to CloudFront filters malicious requests (SQL injection, XSS, bot traffic) before they reach the API. Amazon API Gateway manages the API lifecycle with usage plans defining rate limits and quotas per developer tier, API key management, request/response transformations, and the developer portal for self-service subscription and documentation. AWS Lambda executes the API business logic as serverless functions. Amazon DynamoDB stores API metadata, developer profiles, and usage tracking data with single-digit millisecond performance.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a centralized network inspection architecture that inspects all traffic between VPCs (east-west), all internet-bound traffic (north-south), and all traffic to/from on-premises — using a combination of AWS-native and third-party firewall appliances in an inspection VPC.
toolboxServices: AWS Transit Gateway|AWS Network Firewall|Gateway Load Balancer|Amazon VPC|AWS Firewall Manager|NAT Gateway|AWS Direct Connect
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Transit Gateway|Gateway Load Balancer|AWS Network Firewall|AWS Firewall Manager
hint: Transit Gateway route tables with blackhole routes and appliance mode force all inter-VPC traffic through the centralized inspection VPC.
hint2: Gateway Load Balancer distributes traffic across a fleet of third-party firewall appliances while preserving the original source/destination IPs.
explanation: AWS Transit Gateway centralizes routing with separate route tables for spoke VPCs, inspection VPC, and on-premises (Direct Connect) — appliance mode ensures symmetric routing through the inspection VPC for all east-west, north-south, and hybrid traffic flows. Gateway Load Balancer distributes inspection traffic across a horizontally-scaled fleet of third-party firewall appliances (Palo Alto, Fortinet, Check Point) while transparently preserving original source and destination IP addresses using GENEVE encapsulation. AWS Network Firewall provides the AWS-native stateful inspection layer for domain filtering, IPS/IDS, and protocol-level rules. AWS Firewall Manager centrally deploys and manages both Network Firewall policies and Gateway Load Balancer configurations across all accounts in the organization.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a real-time event-driven architecture that processes 1 million events per second from IoT devices. Events must be filtered, enriched with device metadata, routed to different consumers based on event type, and stored in a time-series database for operational dashboards — all with exactly-once semantics.
toolboxServices: Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|Amazon EventBridge|AWS Lambda|Amazon Timestream|Amazon MSK (Managed Streaming for Apache Kafka)|Amazon DynamoDB|Amazon IoT Core
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon IoT Core|Amazon Kinesis Data Streams|AWS Lambda|Amazon Timestream
hint: IoT Core's rules engine can filter and route messages at ingestion, but for 1M events/second, a dedicated streaming service provides the throughput.
hint2: Kinesis Data Streams with enhanced fan-out provides dedicated 2 MB/s per consumer per shard, supporting multiple independent consumers without contention.
explanation: Amazon IoT Core ingests MQTT/HTTP messages from IoT devices with built-in device authentication, message broker, and basic rules engine filtering. Amazon Kinesis Data Streams receives the high-throughput event stream (1M events/second across hundreds of shards) with enhanced fan-out providing dedicated throughput per consumer for parallel processing. AWS Lambda consumers enrich events with device metadata from DynamoDB, apply business logic transformations, and route events to different downstream services based on event type — with Kinesis exactly-once processing via checkpointing. Amazon Timestream stores the time-series event data with built-in time-based data lifecycle management and provides fast queries for operational dashboards.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a cross-account, cross-region backup strategy for their critical workloads. Backups must be encrypted with customer-managed keys, retained according to regulatory schedules (daily for 30 days, monthly for 1 year, yearly for 7 years), tested automatically for recoverability, and protected from accidental or malicious deletion — including by compromised administrator accounts.
toolboxServices: AWS Backup|AWS Backup Vault Lock|AWS Organizations|AWS KMS|Amazon EventBridge|AWS Lambda|Amazon S3 Object Lock
correctPipelineOrder: AWS Backup|AWS KMS|AWS Backup Vault Lock|Amazon EventBridge|AWS Lambda
hint: AWS Backup with organization-wide backup policies automates backup scheduling and retention across all accounts from a central management account.
hint2: Backup Vault Lock in compliance mode creates a WORM (Write Once Read Many) vault that prevents anyone — including root — from deleting backups before retention expires.
explanation: AWS Backup creates organization-wide backup policies that automatically protect critical workloads across all accounts and regions with lifecycle rules matching regulatory retention schedules (daily/30d, monthly/1y, yearly/7y), copying backups cross-region and cross-account to an isolated vault account. AWS KMS customer-managed keys encrypt all backups with key policies restricting decrypt permissions to authorized recovery roles only. AWS Backup Vault Lock in compliance mode creates immutable WORM vaults that prevent backup deletion by anyone — including root and compromised administrators — until the retention period expires. Amazon EventBridge triggers AWS Lambda functions that automatically perform restore testing on a scheduled basis, validating backup recoverability and reporting results to the operations team.

scenarioDescription: A company needs to implement a cost-optimized architecture for a batch processing workload that runs nightly for 4-6 hours, processing 500 TB of data. The workload is fault-tolerant, can checkpoint progress, and must complete within an 8-hour window. Compute costs must be minimized while guaranteeing the completion SLA.
toolboxServices: Amazon EC2 Spot Instances|Amazon EC2 On-Demand Instances|AWS Batch|Amazon S3|Amazon EBS|EC2 Spot Fleet|AWS Step Functions
correctPipelineOrder: Amazon S3|AWS Batch|EC2 Spot Fleet|AWS Step Functions
hint: AWS Batch manages job queues with mixed Spot and On-Demand compute environments, automatically retrying interrupted Spot jobs.
hint2: Spot Fleet with capacity-optimized allocation strategy selects instance pools with the lowest interruption probability, maximizing job completion rates.
explanation: Amazon S3 stores the 500 TB input dataset and receives processed output, providing durable storage decoupled from compute. AWS Batch manages the job scheduling with a primary Spot compute environment (80% of capacity for cost savings up to 90%) and a fallback On-Demand compute environment that activates if Spot capacity is insufficient to meet the 8-hour SLA. EC2 Spot Fleet uses capacity-optimized allocation strategy across multiple instance types and AZs to minimize interruption probability, with AWS Batch automatically retrying interrupted jobs from their last checkpoint. AWS Step Functions orchestrates the overall nightly workflow — triggering the Batch jobs, monitoring completion, handling failures, and sending completion notifications.
